DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF THE DANGEROUS CONDITION A SUFFICIENT TIME PRIOR TO THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT SO THAT MEASURE COULD HAVE BEEN TAKEN TO PROTECT AGAINST THE DANGEROUS CONDITION. A PUBLIC ENTITY IS NOT LIABLE FOR A DANGEROUS CONDITION WHERE THERE IS NO PROOF EITHER THAT A PUBLIC EMPLOYEE CREATED THE CONDITION OR THAT THE PUBLIC ENTITY HAD ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE KNOWLEDGE THEREOF. VAN KEMPEN V. HAYWARD AREA PARK, ETC., DIST. (1972), 23 CAL. APP. 3D 822, 825. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MEMORANDUM OF COSTS AND DISBURSEMENTS, $5,114.03 TOTAL COSTS, MATURE DATE APR-26-2013, FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bradley A. Russi, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3964, for Defendant and moving party;
John F. Henning, III, Esq. of the Law Offices of Brian Larsen, for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted in its entirety. Plaintiff has failed to provide evidence that Defendant had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition a sufficient time prior to the time of the accident so that measure could have been taken to protect against the dangerous condition. A public entity is not liable for a dangerous condition where there is no proof either that a public employee created the condition or that the public entity had actual or constructive knowledge thereof. Van Kempen v. Hayward Area Park, etc., Dist. (1972), 23 Cal. App. 3d 822, 825. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
